BMM_DEFINITIONS.name: BMM_DEFINITIONS
BMM_DEFINITIONS.documentation: Definitions used by all BMM packages.

Bmm_internal_version.name: Bmm_internal_version
Bmm_internal_version.documentation: Current internal version of BMM meta-model, used to determine if a given schema can be processed by a given implementation of the model.

Schema_name_delimiter.name: Schema_name_delimiter
Schema_name_delimiter.documentation: Delimiter used to separate schema id from package path in a fully qualified path.

Package_name_delimiter.name: Package_name_delimiter
Package_name_delimiter.documentation: Delimiter used to separate package names in a package path.

Type_delimiter.name: Type_delimiter
Type_delimiter.documentation: Appears between a name and a type in a declaration or type signature.

Generic_left_delimiter.name: Generic_left_delimiter
Generic_left_delimiter.documentation: Left delimiter for generic class and generic type names, as used in `List<T>`.

Generic_right_delimiter.name: Generic_right_delimiter
Generic_right_delimiter.documentation: Right delimiter for generic class and generic type names, as used in `List<T>`.

Generic_constraint_delimiter.name: Generic_constraint_delimiter
Generic_constraint_delimiter.documentation: Delimiter between formal type parameter and constraint type, as used in `Sortable<T: Ordered>`.

Tuple_left_delim.name: Tuple_left_delim

Tuple_right_delim.name: Tuple_right_delim
Tuple_right_delim.documentation: Right delimiter of a Tuple type and also instance.

Tuple_separator.name: Tuple_separator
Tuple_separator.documentation: Separator used in Tuple types and instances.

Constraint_left_delim.name: Constraint_left_delim
Constraint_left_delim.documentation: Left delimiter used in serial form of instance constrained enumeration.

Constraint_right_delim.name: Constraint_right_delim
Constraint_right_delim.documentation: Right delimiter used in serial form of instance constrained enumeration.

Metadata_bmm_version.name: Metadata_bmm_version
Metadata_bmm_version.documentation: Attribute name of logical attribute 'bmm_version' in .bmm schema file.

Metadata_schema_name.name: Metadata_schema_name
Metadata_schema_name.documentation: Attribute name of logical attribute 'schema_name' in .bmm schema file.

Metadata_rm_publisher.name: Metadata_rm_publisher
Metadata_rm_publisher.documentation: Attribute name of logical attribute 'rm_publisher' in .bmm schema file.

Metadata_rm_release.name: Metadata_rm_release
Metadata_rm_release.documentation: Attribute name of logical attribute 'rm_release' in .bmm schema file.

Metadata_schema_revision.name: Metadata_schema_revision
Metadata_schema_revision.documentation: Attribute name of logical attribute 'schema_revision' in .bmm schema file.

Metadata_schema_lifecycle_state.name: Metadata_schema_lifecycle_state
Metadata_schema_lifecycle_state.documentation: Attribute name of logical attribute 'schema_lifecycle_state' in .bmm schema file.

Metadata_schema_description.name: Metadata_schema_description
Metadata_schema_description.documentation: Attribute name of logical attribute 'schema_description' in .bmm schema file.

Any_class.name: Any_class
Any_class.documentation: built-in class definition corresponding to the top `Any' class.

Any_type.name: Any_type
Any_type.documentation: Built-in type definition corresponding to the top `Any' type.

create_schema_id.name: create_schema_id
create_schema_id.documentation:
Create schema id, formed from:

`a_model_publisher '-' a_schema_name '-' a_model_release`

e.g. `openehr_rm_1.0.3`, `openehr_test_1.0.1`, `iso_13606_1_2008_2.1.2`.

BMM_MODEL_ACCESS.name: BMM_MODEL_ACCESS
BMM_MODEL_ACCESS.documentation: Access to BMM models that have been loaded and validated from one or more schema sets.

schema_directories.name: schema_directories
schema_directories.documentation: List of directories where all the schemas loaded here are found.

all_schemas.name: all_schemas
all_schemas.documentation: All schemas found and loaded from `_schema_directory_`. Keyed by `_schema_id_`.

bmm_models.name: bmm_models
bmm_models.documentation: Top-level (root) models in use, keyed by `_model_id_`.

matching_bmm_models.name: matching_bmm_models
matching_bmm_models.documentation: Validated models, keyed by `_model_id()_` and any shorter forms of id, with some or no versioning information. For example, the keys `"openEHR_EHR_1.0.4"`, `"openEHR_EHR_1.0"`, `"openEHR_EHR_1"`, and `"openEHR_EHR"` will all match the `"openEHR_EHR_1.0.4"` model, assuming it is the most recent version available.

initialise_with_load_list.name: initialise_with_load_list
initialise_with_load_list.documentation: Initialise with a specific schema load list, usually a sub-set of schemas that will be found in a specified directories `_a_schema_dirs_`.

initialise_all.name: initialise_all
initialise_all.documentation: Load all schemas found in a specified directories `_a_schema_dirs_`.

bmm_model.name: bmm_model
bmm_model.documentation: Return model containing the model key which is a `_model_id_` or any shorter form e.g. model id minus the version. If a shorter key is used, the `BMM_MODEL` with the most recent version will be selected. Uses `_matching_bmm_models_` table to find matches if partial version information is supplied in key.

has_bmm_model.name: has_bmm_model
has_bmm_model.documentation: True if a model for a `_model_key_` is available. A model key is a `_model_id_` or any shorter form e.g. model id minus the version. If a shorter key is used, the Result s True if a `BMM_MODEL` with any version exists.

BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR.name: BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR
BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR.documentation: Descriptor for a BMM schema. Contains a meta-data table of attributes obtained from a mini-ODIN parse of the  schema file.

meta_data.name: meta_data
meta_data.documentation: Table of `{key, value}` of schema meta-data, keys are string values defined by `{BMM_DEFINITIONS}.Metadata_*` constants.

includes.name: includes
includes.documentation: Identifiers of schemas included by this schema.

is_top_level.name: is_top_level
is_top_level.documentation: True if this is a top-level schema, i.e. is the root schema of a 'model'. True if `_bmm_schema_ /= Void and then _bmm_schema.model_name_ /= Void`.

is_bmm_compatible.name: is_bmm_compatible
is_bmm_compatible.documentation: True if the BMM version found in the schema (or assumed, if none) is compatible with that in this software.

load.name: load
load.documentation: Load schema into in-memory form, i.e. a `P_BMM_SCHEMA` instance, if structurally valid. If successful, `_p_schema_` will be set.

validate_merged.name: validate_merged
validate_merged.documentation: Validate loaded schema and report errors.

validate_includes.name: validate_includes
validate_includes.documentation: Validate includes list for this schema, to see if each mentioned schema exists in `_all_schemas_` list.

create_model.name: create_model
create_model.documentation: Create `schema`, i.e. the `BMM_MODEL` from one `P_BMM_SCHEMA` schema.

BMM_SCHEMA.name: BMM_SCHEMA
BMM_SCHEMA.documentation: Abstract parent of any persistable form of a BMM model, e.g. `P_BMM_SCHEMA`.

bmm_version.name: bmm_version
bmm_version.documentation: Version of BMM model, enabling schema evolution reasoning. Persisted attribute.

includes.name: includes
includes.documentation:
Inclusion list of any form of BMM model, in the form of a hash of individual include specifications, each of which at least specifies the id of another schema, and may specify a namespace via which types from the included schemas are known in this schema.
Persisted attribute.

model_name.name: model_name
model_name.documentation: Name of this model, if this schema is a model root point. Not set for sub-schemas that are not considered models on their own.

schema_name.name: schema_name
schema_name.documentation: Name of model expressed in schema; a 'schema' usually contains all of the packages of one 'model' of a publisher. A publisher with more than one model can have multiple schemas.

validate_created.name: validate_created
validate_created.documentation:
Do some basic validation post initial creation

* check that package structure is regular:
** only top-level packages can have qualified names
** no top-level package name can be a direct parent or child of another (child package must be declared under the parent)
* check that all classes are mentioned in the package structure
* check that all models refer to valid packages

load_finalise.name: load_finalise
load_finalise.documentation:
Finalisation work:

* convert packages to canonical form, i.e. full hierarchy with no packages with names like aa.bb.cc
* set up include processing list

merge.name: merge
merge.documentation: Merge in class and package definitions from `_other_`, except where the current schema already has a definition for the given type or package.

validate.name: validate
validate.documentation: Main validation prior to generation of `_bmm_model_`.

read_to_validate.name: read_to_validate
read_to_validate.documentation: True when validation may be commenced.

schema_id.name: schema_id
schema_id.documentation:
Identifier of this schema, used for stating inclusions and identifying files. Formed as:

`{BMM_DEFINITIONS}.create_schema_id ( _rm_publisher_,  _schema_name_,   _rm_release_)`

E.g. `"openehr_rm_ehr_1.0.4"`.

id.name: id
id.documentation: Full identifier of the included schema, e.g. `"openehr_primitive_types_1.0.2"`.

BMM_SCHEMA_STATE.name: BMM_SCHEMA_STATE
BMM_SCHEMA_STATE.documentation: Enumeration of processing states of a `BMM_SCHEMA` used by creation and validation routines in `BMM_SCHEMA`.
item_documentations: Initial state directly after instantiation of schema.
State of schema processing if there are still included schemas to process.
State when all included schemas have been processed.

BMM_MODEL_ELEMENT.name: BMM_MODEL_ELEMENT
BMM_MODEL_ELEMENT.documentation: Abstract meta-type of BMM declared model elements. A _declaration_ is a an element of a model within a context, which defines the _scope_ of the element. Thus, a class definition and its property and routine definitions are model elements, but Types are not, since they are derived from model elements.

documentation.name: documentation
documentation.documentation:
Optional documentation of this element, as a keyed list.

It is strongly recommended to use the following key /type combinations for the relevant purposes:

* `"purpose": String`
* `"keywords": List<String>`
* `"use": String`
* `"misuse": String`
* `"references": String`

Other keys and value types may be freely added.

scope.name: scope
scope.documentation: Model element within which an element is declared.

extensions.name: extensions
extensions.documentation: Optional meta-data of this element, as a keyed list. May be used to extend the meta-model.

is_root_scope.name: is_root_scope
is_root_scope.documentation: True if this model element is the root of a model structure hierarchy.

BMM_FORMAL_ELEMENT.name: BMM_FORMAL_ELEMENT
BMM_FORMAL_ELEMENT.documentation: A formal element having a name, type and a type-based signature.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Declared or inferred static type of the entity.

is_nullable.name: is_nullable
is_nullable.documentation: True if this element can be null (Void) at execution time. May be interpreted as optionality in subtypes..

signature.name: signature
signature.documentation:
Formal signature of this element, in the form:

`name [arg1_name: T_arg1, ...][:T_value]`

Specific implementations in descendants.

is_boolean.name: is_boolean
is_boolean.documentation: True if `_type_` is notionally Boolean (i.e. a `BMM_SIMPLE_TYPE` with `_type_name()_` = `'Boolean'`).

BMM_CONSTANT.name: BMM_CONSTANT
BMM_CONSTANT.documentation: An immutable, static value-returning element scoped to a class. The `_value_` is the result of the evaluation of the `_generator_`, which may be as simple as a literal value, or may be any expression, including a function call.

BMM_CONTAINER_PROPERTY.name: BMM_CONTAINER_PROPERTY
BMM_CONTAINER_PROPERTY.documentation: Meta-type of for properties of linear container type, such as List<T> etc.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Declared or inferred static type of the entity.

display_name.name: display_name
display_name.documentation: Name of this property in form `name: ContainerTypeName<>`.

BMM_FUNCTION.name: BMM_FUNCTION
BMM_FUNCTION.documentation: A formal element with signature of the form: `name ({arg:TArg}*):TResult`. A function is a computed (rather than data) element, generally assumed to be non-state-changing.

operator_definition.name: operator_definition
operator_definition.documentation: Optional details enabling a function to be represented as an operator in a syntactic representation.

result.name: result
result.documentation: Automatically created Result variable, usable in body and post-condition.

BMM_OPERATOR.name: BMM_OPERATOR
BMM_OPERATOR.documentation: Definition of a symbolic operator associated with a function.

position.name: position
position.documentation: Position of operator in syntactic representation.

symbols.name: symbols
symbols.documentation: Set of `String` symbols that may be used to represent this operator in a textual representation of a BMM model.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Formal name of the operator, e.g. 'minus' etc.

BMM_PROPERTY.name: BMM_PROPERTY
BMM_PROPERTY.documentation: Meta-type of a writable property definition within a class definition of an object model. The `_is_composition_` attribute indicates whether the property has sub-part or an association semantics with respect to the owning class.

is_im_runtime.name: is_im_runtime
is_im_runtime.documentation: True if this property is marked with info model `_im_runtime_` property.

is_im_infrastructure.name: is_im_infrastructure
is_im_infrastructure.documentation: True if this property was marked with info model `_im_infrastructure_` flag.

is_composition.name: is_composition
is_composition.documentation: True if this property instance is a compositional sub-part of the owning class instance. Equivalent to 'composition' in UML associations (but missing from UML properties without associations) and also 'cascade-delete' semantics in ER schemas.

existence.name: existence
existence.documentation: Interval form of `0..1`, `1..1` etc, derived from `_is_nullable_`.

display_name.name: display_name
display_name.documentation: Name of this property to display in UI.

BMM_ROUTINE.name: BMM_ROUTINE
BMM_ROUTINE.documentation: A feature defining a routine, scoped to a class.

pre_conditions.name: pre_conditions
pre_conditions.documentation:
Boolean conditions that must evaluate to True for the routine to execute correctly, May be used to generate exceptions if included in run-time build.

A False pre-condition implies an error in the passed parameters.

post_conditions.name: post_conditions
post_conditions.documentation:
Boolean conditions that will evaluate to True if the routine executed correctly, May be used to generate exceptions if included in run-time build.

A False post-condition implies an error (i.e. bug) in routine code.

definition.name: definition
definition.documentation: Body of a routine, i.e. executable program.

arity.name: arity
arity.documentation: Return number of arguments of this routine.

direction.name: direction
direction.documentation: Optional read/write direction of the parameter. If none-supplied, the parameter is treated as `in`, i.e. readable.

scope.name: scope
scope.documentation: Routine within which variable is defined.

BMM_PROCEDURE.name: BMM_PROCEDURE
BMM_PROCEDURE.documentation: A formal element with signature of the form: `name ({arg:TArg}*):TStatus`, where `TStatus` is the built-in type `BMM_STATUS_TYPE`.. A procedure is a computed (rather than data) element, generally assumed to be state-changing, and is usually called in the form `name ({arg:TArg}*)`.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Declared or inferred static type of the entity.

signature.name: signature
signature.documentation:
Formal signature of this element, in the form:

`name [arg1_name: T_arg1, ...][:T_value]`

Specific implementations in descendants.

BMM_UNITARY_PROPERTY.name: BMM_UNITARY_PROPERTY
BMM_UNITARY_PROPERTY.documentation: Meta-type of for properties of unitary type.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Declared or inferred static type of the entity.

BMM_INDEXED_CONTAINER_PROPERTY.name: BMM_INDEXED_CONTAINER_PROPERTY
BMM_INDEXED_CONTAINER_PROPERTY.documentation: Meta-type of for properties of linear container type, such as `Hash<Index_type, T>` etc.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Declared or inferred static type of the entity.

display_name.name: display_name
display_name.documentation: Name of this property in form `name: ContainerTypeName<IndexTypeName, ...>`.

BMM_INSTANTIABLE_FEATURE.name: BMM_INSTANTIABLE_FEATURE
BMM_INSTANTIABLE_FEATURE.documentation: Meta-type representing instantiable features, i.e. features that are created as value objects.

BMM_RESULT.name: BMM_RESULT
BMM_RESULT.documentation: Automatically declared variable representing result of a Function call (writable).

BMM_ROUTINE_DEFINITION.name: BMM_ROUTINE_DEFINITION
BMM_ROUTINE_DEFINITION.documentation: Abstract ancestor of routine body meta-types.

BMM_EXTERNAL_ROUTINE.name: BMM_EXTERNAL_ROUTINE
BMM_EXTERNAL_ROUTINE.documentation: External routine meta-type, containing sufficient meta-data to enable a routine in an external library to be called.

meta_data.name: meta_data
meta_data.documentation: External call general meta-data, including target routine name, type mapping etc.

BMM_FEATURE_GROUP.name: BMM_FEATURE_GROUP
BMM_FEATURE_GROUP.documentation: A logical group of features, with a name and set of properties that applies to the group.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name of this feature group; defaults to 'feature'.

properties.name: properties
properties.documentation: Set of properties of this group, represented as name/value pairs. These are understood to apply logically to all of the features contained within the group.

features.name: features
features.documentation: Set of features in this group.

visibility.name: visibility
visibility.documentation: Optional visibility to apply to all features in this group.

BMM_VISIBILITY.name: BMM_VISIBILITY
BMM_VISIBILITY.documentation:
Abstract parent of visibility representation.

TODO: define schemes; probably need to support C++/Java scheme as well as better type-based schemes.

is_synthesised_generic.name: is_synthesised_generic
is_synthesised_generic.documentation: True if this feature was synthesised due to generic substitution in an inherited type, or further constraining of a formal generic parameter.

scope.name: scope
scope.documentation: Model element within which an element is declared.

BMM_SELF.name: BMM_SELF
BMM_SELF.documentation: Meta-type for an automatically created variable referencing the current instance. Typically called 'self' or 'this' in programming languages. Read-only.

BMM_WRITABLE_VARIABLE.name: BMM_WRITABLE_VARIABLE
BMM_WRITABLE_VARIABLE.documentation: Meta-type for writable variables, including the special variable `Result`.

BMM_READONLY_VARIABLE.name: BMM_READONLY_VARIABLE
BMM_READONLY_VARIABLE.documentation: Meta-type for writable variables, including routine parameters and the special variable `Self`.

BMM_SINGLETON.name: BMM_SINGLETON
BMM_SINGLETON.documentation: Meta-type for static value properties computed once by a function invocation.

generator.name: generator
generator.documentation: Generator of the value of this static property.

BMM_LOCAL_ROUTINE.name: BMM_LOCAL_ROUTINE
BMM_LOCAL_ROUTINE.documentation: Meta-type for locally declared routine body.

locals.name: locals
locals.documentation: Local variables of the routine, if there is a body defined.

BMM_OPERATOR_POSITION.name: BMM_OPERATOR_POSITION
BMM_OPERATOR_POSITION.documentation: Enumeration of possible position of operator in a syntactic representation for operators associated with 1- and 2- degree functions.
item_documentations: Prefix operator position: operator comes before operand.
Infix operator position: operator comes between left and right operands.

BMM_PARAMETER_DIRECTION.name: BMM_PARAMETER_DIRECTION
BMM_PARAMETER_DIRECTION.documentation: Enumeration of parameter read/write direction values.
item_documentations: Parameter is an input parameter, and treated as readonly by the receiving routine.
Parameter is an output parameter, and treated as a reference to an entity writeable by the receiving routine.
Parameter is an input and output parameter, and treated as a reference to an entity readable and writeable by the receiving routine.

BMM_INTEGER_VALUE.name: BMM_INTEGER_VALUE
BMM_INTEGER_VALUE.documentation: Meta-type for a literal Integer value, for which `_type_` is fixed to the `BMM_TYPE` representing `Integer` and `_value_` is of type `Integer`.

BMM_STRING_VALUE.name: BMM_STRING_VALUE
BMM_STRING_VALUE.documentation: Meta-type for a literal String value, for which `_type_` is fixed to the `BMM_TYPE` representing `String` and `_value_` is of type `String`.

BMM_LITERAL_VALUE.name: BMM_LITERAL_VALUE
BMM_LITERAL_VALUE.documentation: Meta-type for literal instance values declared in a model. Instance values may be inline values of primitive types in the usual fashion or complex objects in syntax form, e.g. JSON.

value_literal.name: value_literal
value_literal.documentation: A serial representation of the value.

value.name: value
value.documentation: A native representation of the value, possibly derived by deserialising `_value_literal_`.

syntax.name: syntax
syntax.documentation: Optional specification of formalism of the `_value_literal_` attribute for complex values. Value may be any of `json | json5 | yawl | xml | odin | rdf` or another value agreed by the user community. If not set, `json` is assumed.

BMM_BOOLEAN_VALUE.name: BMM_BOOLEAN_VALUE
BMM_BOOLEAN_VALUE.documentation: Meta-type for a literal Boolean value, for which `_type_` is fixed to the `BMM_TYPE` representing `Boolean` and `_value_` is of type `Boolean`.

BMM_PRIMITIVE_VALUE.name: BMM_PRIMITIVE_VALUE
BMM_PRIMITIVE_VALUE.documentation: Meta-type for literals whose concrete type is a primitive type.

BMM_CONTAINER_VALUE.name: BMM_CONTAINER_VALUE
BMM_CONTAINER_VALUE.documentation: Meta-type for literals whose concrete type is a linear container type, i.e. array, list or set.

BMM_UNITARY_VALUE.name: BMM_UNITARY_VALUE
BMM_UNITARY_VALUE.documentation: Meta-type for literals whose concrete type is a unitary type in the BMM sense.

BMM_INDEXED_CONTAINER_VALUE.name: BMM_INDEXED_CONTAINER_VALUE
BMM_INDEXED_CONTAINER_VALUE.documentation: Meta-type for literals whose concrete type is an indexed container, i.e. Hash table, Map etc.

BMM_INTERVAL_VALUE.name: BMM_INTERVAL_VALUE
BMM_INTERVAL_VALUE.documentation: Meta-type for literal intervals of type `Interval<Ordered>`.

BMM_MODEL.name: BMM_MODEL
BMM_MODEL.documentation: Definition of the root of a BMM model (along with what is inherited from `BMM_SCHEMA_CORE`).

class_definitions.name: class_definitions
class_definitions.documentation: All classes in this model, keyed by type name.

used_models.name: used_models
used_models.documentation: List of other models 'used' (i.e. 'imported' by this model). Classes in the current model may refer to classes in a used model by specifying the other class's `_scope_` meta-attribute.

modules.name: modules
modules.documentation: All classes in this model, keyed by type name.

model_id.name: model_id
model_id.documentation:
Identifier of this model, lower-case, formed from:

`<rm_publisher>_<model_name>_<rm_release>`

E.g. `"openehr_ehr_1.0.4"`.

class_definition.name: class_definition
class_definition.documentation: Retrieve the class definition corresponding to `_a_type_name_` (which may contain a generic part).

type_definition.name: type_definition
type_definition.documentation: Retrieve the class definition corresponding to `_a_type_name_`. If it contains a generic part, this will be removed if it is a fully open generic name, otherwise it will remain intact, i.e. if it is an effective generic name that identifies a `BMM_GENERIC_CLASS_EFFECTIVE`.

has_class_definition.name: has_class_definition
has_class_definition.documentation: True if `_a_class_name_` has a class definition in the model.

has_type_definition.name: has_type_definition
has_type_definition.documentation: True if `_a_type_name_` is already concretely known in the system, including if it is generic, which may be open, partially open or closed.

enumeration_definition.name: enumeration_definition
enumeration_definition.documentation: Retrieve the enumeration definition corresponding to `_a_type_name_`.

primitive_types.name: primitive_types
primitive_types.documentation: List of keys in `_class_definitions_` of items marked as primitive types.

enumeration_types.name: enumeration_types
enumeration_types.documentation: List of keys in `_class_definitions_` of items that are enumeration types.

property_definition.name: property_definition
property_definition.documentation: Retrieve the property definition for `_a_prop_name_` in flattened class corresponding to `_a_type_name_`.

ms_conformant_property_type.name: ms_conformant_property_type
ms_conformant_property_type.documentation: True if `_a_ms_property_type_` is a valid 'MS' dynamic type for `_a_property_` in BMM type `_a_bmm_type_name_`. 'MS' conformance means 'model-semantic' conformance, which abstracts away container types like `List<>`, `Set<>` etc and compares the dynamic type with the relation target type in the UML sense, i.e. regardless of whether there is single or multiple containment.

property_definition_at_path.name: property_definition_at_path
property_definition_at_path.documentation: Retrieve the property definition for `_a_property_path_` in flattened class corresponding to `_a_type_name_`.

class_definition_at_path.name: class_definition_at_path
class_definition_at_path.documentation: Retrieve the class definition for the class that owns the terminal attribute in `a_prop_path` in flattened class corresponding to `a_type_name`.

all_ancestor_classes.name: all_ancestor_classes
all_ancestor_classes.documentation: Return all ancestor types of `_a_class_name_` up to root class (usually `Any`, `Object` or something similar). Does  not include current class. Returns empty list if none.

is_descendant_of.name: is_descendant_of
is_descendant_of.documentation: True if `_a_class_name_` is a descendant in the model of `_a_parent_class_name_`.

type_conforms_to.name: type_conforms_to
type_conforms_to.documentation:
Check conformance of `_a_desc_type_` to `_an_anc_type_`; the types may be generic, and may contain open generic parameters like 'T' etc. These are replaced with their appropriate constrainer types, or Any during the conformance testing process.

Conformance is found if:

* [base class test] types are non-generic, and either type names are identical, or else `_a_desc_type_` has `_an_anc_type_` in its ancestors;
* both types are generic and pass base class test; number of generic params matches, and each generic parameter type, after 'open parameter' substitution, recursively passes; `_type_name_conforms_to_` test
* descendant type is generic and ancestor type is not, and they pass base classes test.

subtypes.name: subtypes
subtypes.documentation: Generate type substitutions for the supplied type, which may be simple, generic (closed, open or partially open), or a container type. In the generic and container cases, the result is the permutation of the base class type and type substitutions of all generic parameters.

any_class_definition.name: any_class_definition
any_class_definition.documentation: `BMM_SIMPLE_CLASS` instance for the `Any` class. This may be defined in the BMM schema, but if not, use `BMM_DEFINITIONS._any_class_`.

BMM_MODEL_METADATA.name: BMM_MODEL_METADATA
BMM_MODEL_METADATA.documentation: Core properties of `BMM_MODEL`, may be used in a serial representation as well, such as `P_BMM_SCHEMA`.

rm_publisher.name: rm_publisher
rm_publisher.documentation: Publisher of model expressed in the schema.

rm_release.name: rm_release
rm_release.documentation: Release of model expressed in the schema as a 3-part numeric, e.g. "3.1.0" .

BMM_PACKAGE.name: BMM_PACKAGE
BMM_PACKAGE.documentation:
Abstraction of a package as a tree structure whose nodes can contain other packages and classes.

The `_name_` may be qualified if it is a top-level package.

root_classes.name: root_classes
root_classes.documentation: Obtain the set of top-level classes in this package, either from this package itself or by recursing into the structure until classes are obtained from child packages. Recurse into each child only far enough to find the first level of classes.

path.name: path
path.documentation: Full path of this package back to root package.

BMM_PACKAGE_CONTAINER.name: BMM_PACKAGE_CONTAINER
BMM_PACKAGE_CONTAINER.documentation: A BMM model component that contains packages and classes.

packages.name: packages
packages.documentation: Child packages; keys all in upper case for guaranteed matching.

scope.name: scope
scope.documentation: Model element within which a referenceable element is known.

do_recursive_packages.name: do_recursive_packages
do_recursive_packages.documentation: Recursively execute `_action_`, which is a procedure taking a `BMM_PACKAGE` argument, on all members of packages.

has_package_path.name: has_package_path
has_package_path.documentation: True if there is a package at the path `_a_path_`; paths are delimited with delimiter `{BMM_DEFINITIONS} _Package_name_delimiter_`.

EL_EXPRESSION.name: EL_EXPRESSION
EL_EXPRESSION.documentation: Abstract parent of all typed expression meta-types.

eval_type.name: eval_type
eval_type.documentation:
Meta-type of expression entity used in type-checking and evaluation. 

Effected in descendants.

is_boolean.name: is_boolean
is_boolean.documentation: True if `_eval_type_` is notionally Boolean (i.e. a `BMM_SIMPLE_TYPE` with `_type_name()_` = `Boolean`).

EL_LITERAL.name: EL_LITERAL
EL_LITERAL.documentation: Literal value of any type known in the model, including primitive types. Defined via a `BMM_LITERAL_VALUE`.

value.name: value
value.documentation: The reference item from which the value of this node can be computed.

precedence_overridden.name: precedence_overridden
precedence_overridden.documentation: True if the natural precedence of operators is overridden in the expression represented by this node of the expression tree. If True, parentheses should be introduced around the totality of the syntax expression corresponding to this operator node and its operands.

symbol.name: symbol
symbol.documentation: The symbol actually used in the expression, or intended to be used for serialisation. Must be a member of `OPERATOR_DEF._symbols_`.

call.name: call
call.documentation: Function call equivalent to this operator expression, inferred by matching operator against functions defined in interface of principal operand.

equivalent_call.name: equivalent_call
equivalent_call.documentation: Function call equivalent to this operator.

eval_type.name: eval_type
eval_type.documentation: Type definition (i.e. BMM meta-type definition object) of the constant, property or variable, inferred by inspection of the current scoping instance. Return `_definition.type_`.

EL_PREDICATE.name: EL_PREDICATE
EL_PREDICATE.documentation: Parent type of predicate of any object reference.

operand.name: operand
operand.documentation: The target instance of this predicate.

EL_DEFINED.name: EL_DEFINED
EL_DEFINED.documentation:
A predicate taking one external variable reference argument, that returns true if the reference is resolvable, i.e. the external value is obtainable.

NOTE: probably to be removed.

EL_FUNCTION_CALL.name: EL_FUNCTION_CALL
EL_FUNCTION_CALL.documentation: A call made on a closed function agent, returning a result. Equivalent to an 'application' of a function in Lambda calculus.

reference.name: reference
reference.documentation: Generated full reference name, consisting of any scoping elements, function name and routine parameters enclosed in parentheses.

EL_AGENT_CALL.name: EL_AGENT_CALL
EL_AGENT_CALL.documentation: A call made to a 'closed' agent, i.e. one with no remaining open arguments.

EL_AGENT.name: EL_AGENT
EL_AGENT.documentation:
A delayed routine call, whose arguments may be open, partially closed or closed. Generated by special reference to a routine, usually via a dedicated keyword, such as 'lambda' or 'agent', or other special syntax.

Instances may include closed delayed calls like `calculate_age (dob="1987-09-13", today="2019-06-03")` but also partially open calls such as `format_structure (struct=?, style=3)`, where `struct` is an open argument.

Evaluation type (i.e. type of runtime evaluated form) is `BMM_SIGNATURE`.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name of the routine being called.

closed_args.name: closed_args
closed_args.documentation: Closed arguments of a routine call as a tuple of objects.

open_args.name: open_args
open_args.documentation: Optional list of names of open arguments of the call. If not provided, and the `_name_` refers to a routine with more arguments than supplied in `_closed_args_`, the missing arguments are inferred from the `_definition_`.

definition.name: definition
definition.documentation: Reference to definition of a routine for which this is an agent, if one exists.

eval_type.name: eval_type
eval_type.documentation: Eval type is the signature corresponding to the (remaining) open arguments and return type, if any.

is_callable.name: is_callable
is_callable.documentation: True if there are no open arguments.

reference.name: reference
reference.documentation: Generated full reference name, including scoping elements.

EL_TUPLE_ITEM.name: EL_TUPLE_ITEM
EL_TUPLE_ITEM.documentation: A single tuple item, with an optional name.

item.name: item
item.documentation: Reference to value entity. If Void, this indicates that the item in this position is Void, e.g. within a routine call parameter list.

EL_PROCEDURE_AGENT.name: EL_PROCEDURE_AGENT
EL_PROCEDURE_AGENT.documentation: An agent whose signature is of a procedure, i.e. has no result type.

definition.name: definition
definition.documentation: Reference to definition of routine for which this is a call instance.

eval_type.name: eval_type
eval_type.documentation: Eval type is the signature corresponding to the (remaining) open arguments and return type, if any.

EL_TUPLE.name: EL_TUPLE
EL_TUPLE.documentation: Defines an array of optionally named items each of any type.

items.name: items
items.documentation: Items in the tuple, potentially with names. Typical use is to represent an argument list to routine call.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Static type inferred from literal value.

EL_FUNCTION_AGENT.name: EL_FUNCTION_AGENT
EL_FUNCTION_AGENT.documentation: An agent whose signature is of a function, i.e. has a result type.

definition.name: definition
definition.documentation: Reference to definition of a routine for which this is a direct call instance, if one exists.

eval_type.name: eval_type
eval_type.documentation: Eval type is the signature corresponding to the (remaining) open arguments and return type, if any.

EL_ATTACHED.name: EL_ATTACHED
EL_ATTACHED.documentation: A predicate on any object reference (including function call) that returns True if the reference is attached, i.e. non-Void.

EL_VALUE_GENERATOR.name: EL_VALUE_GENERATOR
EL_VALUE_GENERATOR.documentation: Meta-type representing a value-generating simple expression.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name used to represent the reference or other entity.

reference.name: reference
reference.documentation: Generated full reference name, based on constituent parts of the entity. Default version outputs `_name_` field.

EL_TYPE_REF.name: EL_TYPE_REF
EL_TYPE_REF.documentation:
Meta-type for reference to a non-abstract type as an object. Assumed to be accessible at run-time. Typically represented syntactically as `TypeName` or `{TypeName}`.

May be used as a value, or as the qualifier for a function or constant access.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Type, directly from the name of the reference, e.g. `{SOME_TYPE}`.

EL_FEATURE_REF.name: EL_FEATURE_REF
EL_FEATURE_REF.documentation: A reference that is scoped by a containing entity and requires a context qualifier if it is not the currently scoping entity.

scoper.name: scoper
scoper.documentation: Scoping expression, which must be a `EL_VALUE_GENERATOR`.

reference.name: reference
reference.documentation: Generated full reference name, consisting of scoping elements and `_name_` concatenated using dot notation.

EL_CONDITIONAL_EXPRESSION.name: EL_CONDITIONAL_EXPRESSION
EL_CONDITIONAL_EXPRESSION.documentation: Conditional structure used in condition chain expressions. Evaluated by evaluating its `_condition_`, which is a Boolean-returning expression, and if this returns True, the result is the evaluation result of `_expression_`.

condition.name: condition
condition.documentation: Boolean expression defining the condition of this decision branch.

EL_CONDITION_CHAIN.name: EL_CONDITION_CHAIN
EL_CONDITION_CHAIN.documentation:
Compound expression consisting of a chain of condition-gated expressions, and an ungated `_else_` member that as a whole, represents an if/then/elseif/else chains.

Evaluated by iterating through `_items_` and for each one, evaluating its `_condition_`, which if True, causes the evaluation result of the chain to be that item's `_result_` evaluation result.

If no member of `_items_` has a True-returning `_condition_`, the evaluation result is the result of evaluating the `_else_` expression.

items.name: items
items.documentation: Members of the chain, equivalent to branches in an if/then/else chain and cases in a case statement.

EL_CONSTRAINED.name: EL_CONSTRAINED
EL_CONSTRAINED.documentation: Abstract parent for second-order constrained forms of first-order expression meta-types.

base_expression.name: base_expression
base_expression.documentation: The base expression of this constrained form.

EL_CASE_TABLE.name: EL_CASE_TABLE
EL_CASE_TABLE.documentation:
Compound expression consisting of a list of value-range / expression pairs, and an  `_else_` member that as a whole, represents a case statement flavour of decision table.

Evaluated by iterating through `_items_` and for each one, comparing `_input_` to the item `_value_range_`. If the `_input_` is in the range, the evaluation result of the table is that item's `_result_` evaluation result. 

If no member of `_items_` has a True-returning `_condition_`, the evaluation result is the result of evaluating the `_else_` expression.

items.name: items
items.documentation: Members of the chain, equivalent to branches in an if/then/else chain and cases in a case statement.

test_value.name: test_value
test_value.documentation: Expressing generating the input value for the case table.

EL_CASE.name: EL_CASE
EL_CASE.documentation: One branch of a Case table, consisting of a value constraint (the match criterion) and a result, of the generic parameter type T.

EL_DECISION_TABLE.name: EL_DECISION_TABLE
EL_DECISION_TABLE.documentation: Meta-type for decision tables. Generic on the meta-type of the `_result_` attribute of the branches, to allow specialised forms of if/else and case structures to be created.

items.name: items
items.documentation: Members of the chain, equivalent to branches in an if/then/else chain and cases in a case statement.

else.name: else
else.documentation: Result expression of conditional, if its `_condition_` evaluates to True.

EL_DECISION_BRANCH.name: EL_DECISION_BRANCH
EL_DECISION_BRANCH.documentation: Abstract parent of meta-types representing a branch of some kind of decision structure. Defines `result` as being of the generic type `T`.

result.name: result
result.documentation: Result expression of conditional, if its `_condition_` evaluates to True.

EL_VARIABLE.name: EL_VARIABLE
EL_VARIABLE.documentation: Abstract meta-type of any kind of symbolic variable.

EL_TERMINAL.name: EL_TERMINAL
EL_TERMINAL.documentation: Expression entities that are terminals (i.e. leaves) within operator expressions or tuples.

EL_SIMPLE.name: EL_SIMPLE
EL_SIMPLE.documentation: Simple terminal i.e. logically atomic expression element.

EL_STATIC_REF.name: EL_STATIC_REF
EL_STATIC_REF.documentation: Reference to a writable property, either a constant or computed.

EL_WRITABLE_VARIABLE.name: EL_WRITABLE_VARIABLE
EL_WRITABLE_VARIABLE.documentation: Meta-type of writable variables, including routine locals and the special variable 'Result'.

definition.name: definition
definition.documentation: Variable definition to which this reference refers.

is_writable.name: is_writable
is_writable.documentation: Defined to return True in all cases.

EL_READONLY_VARIABLE.name: EL_READONLY_VARIABLE
EL_READONLY_VARIABLE.documentation: Meta-type of read-only variables, including routine parameter and the special variable 'Self'.

definition.name: definition
definition.documentation: Variable definition to which this reference refers.

is_writable.name: is_writable
is_writable.documentation: Defined to return False in all cases.

BMM_ACTION_DECISION_TABLE.name: BMM_ACTION_DECISION_TABLE
BMM_ACTION_DECISION_TABLE.documentation: Specialised form of Decision Table that allows only procedure call agents (lambdas) as the result of branches.

decision_table.name: decision_table
decision_table.documentation: A specialised decision table whose outputs can only be procedure agents. In execution, the matched agent will be invoked.

BMM_ASSIGNMENT.name: BMM_ASSIGNMENT
BMM_ASSIGNMENT.documentation:
Statement type representing an assignment from a value-generating source to a writable entity, i.e. a variable reference or property.

At the meta-model level, may be understood as an initialisation of an existing meta-model instance.

target.name: target
target.documentation: The target variable on the notional left-hand side of this assignment.

source.name: source
source.documentation: Source right hand side) of the assignment.

BMM_ASSERTION.name: BMM_ASSERTION
BMM_ASSERTION.documentation:
A statement that asserts the truth of its expression. If the expression evaluates to False the execution generates an exception (depending on run-time settings).

May be rendered in syntax as `assert condition` or similar.

tag.name: tag
tag.documentation: Optional tag, typically used to designate design intention of the assertion, e.g. `"Inv_all_members_valid"`.

BMM_PROCEDURE_CALL.name: BMM_PROCEDURE_CALL
BMM_PROCEDURE_CALL.documentation: A call made on a closed procedure agent. The method in BMM via which external actions are achieved from within a program.

BMM_STATEMENT_ITEM.name: BMM_STATEMENT_ITEM
BMM_STATEMENT_ITEM.documentation: Abstract parent of statement types representing a locally defined routine body.

BMM_STATEMENT.name: BMM_STATEMENT
BMM_STATEMENT.documentation: Abstract parent of 'statement' types that may be defined to implement BMM Routines.

BMM_STATEMENT_BLOCK.name: BMM_STATEMENT_BLOCK
BMM_STATEMENT_BLOCK.documentation: A statement 'block' corresponding to the programming language concept of the same name. May be used to establish scope in specific languages.

items.name: items
items.documentation: Child blocks of the current block.

BMM_SIMPLE_STATEMENT.name: BMM_SIMPLE_STATEMENT
BMM_SIMPLE_STATEMENT.documentation: Simple statement, i.e. statement with one logical element - a single expression, procedure call etc.

BMM_DECLARATION.name: BMM_DECLARATION
BMM_DECLARATION.documentation: Declaration of a writable variable, associating a name with a type.

type.name: type
type.documentation: The declared type of the variable.

P_BMM_CLASS.name: P_BMM_CLASS
P_BMM_CLASS.documentation: Definition of persistent form of `BMM_CLASS` for serialisation to ODIN, JSON, XML etc.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name of the class. Persisted attribute.

ancestors.name: ancestors
ancestors.documentation: List of immediate inheritance parents. If there are generic ancestors, use `_ancestor_defs_` instead. Persisted attribute.

properties.name: properties
properties.documentation: List of attributes defined in this class. Persistent attribute.

is_abstract.name: is_abstract
is_abstract.documentation: True if this is an abstract type. Persisted attribute.

is_override.name: is_override
is_override.documentation: True if this class definition overrides one found in an included schema.

generic_parameter_defs.name: generic_parameter_defs
generic_parameter_defs.documentation: List of generic parameter definitions. Persisted attribute.

source_schema_id.name: source_schema_id
source_schema_id.documentation: Reference to original source schema defining this class. Set during `BMM_SCHEMA` materialise. Useful for GUI tools to enable user to edit the schema file containing a given class (i.e. taking into account that a class may be in any of the schemas in a schema inclusion hierarchy).

uid.name: uid
uid.documentation: Unique id generated for later comparison during merging, in order to detect if two classes are the same. Assigned in post-load processing.

ancestor_defs.name: ancestor_defs
ancestor_defs.documentation: List of structured inheritance ancestors, used only in the case of generic inheritance. Persisted attribute.

is_generic.name: is_generic
is_generic.documentation: True if this class is a generic class.

populate_bmm_class.name: populate_bmm_class
populate_bmm_class.documentation: Add remaining model elements from Current to `_bmm_class_definition_`.

canonical_packages.name: canonical_packages
canonical_packages.documentation: Package structure in which all top-level qualified package names like `xx.yy.zz` have been expanded out to a hierarchy of `BMM_PACKAGE` objects.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name of this property within its class. Persisted attribute.

is_mandatory.name: is_mandatory
is_mandatory.documentation: True if this property is mandatory in its class. Persisted attribute.

is_computed.name: is_computed
is_computed.documentation: True if this property is computed rather than stored in objects of this class. Persisted Attribute.

is_im_infrastructure.name: is_im_infrastructure
is_im_infrastructure.documentation: True if this property is info model 'infrastructure' rather than 'data'. Persisted attribute.

is_im_runtime.name: is_im_runtime
is_im_runtime.documentation: True if this property is info model 'runtime' settable property. Persisted attribute.

type_def.name: type_def
type_def.documentation: Type definition of this property, if not a simple String type reference. Persisted attribute.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name of the parameter, e.g. 'T' etc. Persisted attribute. Name is limited to 1 character, upper case.

conforms_to_type.name: conforms_to_type
conforms_to_type.documentation: Optional conformance constraint - the name of a type to which a concrete substitution of this generic parameter must conform. Persisted attribute.

create_bmm_type.name: create_bmm_type
create_bmm_type.documentation: Create appropriate `BMM_XXX` object; effected in descendants.

as_type_string.name: as_type_string
as_type_string.documentation: Formal name of the type for display.

container_type.name: container_type
container_type.documentation: The type of the container. This converts to the `_root_type_` in `BMM_GENERIC_TYPE`. Persisted attribute.

type_def.name: type_def
type_def.documentation: Type definition of `_type_`, if not a simple String type reference. Persisted attribute.

type.name: type
type.documentation: The target type; this converts to the first parameter in `_generic_parameters_` in `BMM_GENERIC_TYPE`. Persisted attribute.

type_ref.name: type_ref
type_ref.documentation: The target type; this converts to the first parameter in `_generic_parameters_` in `BMM_GENERIC_TYPE`. Persisted attribute.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Name of type - must be a simple class name.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Simple type parameter as a single letter like 'T', 'G' etc.

root_type.name: root_type
root_type.documentation: Root type of this generic type, e.g. `Interval` in `Interval<Integer>`.

generic_parameter_defs.name: generic_parameter_defs
generic_parameter_defs.documentation: Generic parameters of the root_type in this type specifier if non-simple types. The order must match the order of the owning class's formal generic parameter declarations. Persistent attribute.

generic_parameters.name: generic_parameters
generic_parameters.documentation: Generic parameters of the `_root_type_` in this type specifier, if simple types. The order must match the order of the owning class's formal generic parameter declarations. Persistent attribute.

generic_parameter_refs.name: generic_parameter_refs
generic_parameter_refs.documentation: Generic parameters of the root_type in this type specifier. The order must match the order of the owning class's formal generic parameter declarations

P_BMM_PACKAGE.name: P_BMM_PACKAGE
P_BMM_PACKAGE.documentation: Persisted form of a package as a tree structure whose nodes can contain more packages and/or classes.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name of the package from schema; this name may be qualified if it is a top-level package within the schema, or unqualified. Persistent attribute.

classes.name: classes
classes.documentation: List of classes in this package. Persistent attribute.

merge.name: merge
merge.documentation: Merge packages and classes from other (from an included `P_BMM_SCHEMA`) into this package.

create_bmm_package_definition.name: create_bmm_package_definition
create_bmm_package_definition.documentation: Generate a `BMM_PACKAGE_DEFINITION` object and write it to `_bmm_package_definition_`.

P_BMM_PACKAGE_CONTAINER.name: P_BMM_PACKAGE_CONTAINER
P_BMM_PACKAGE_CONTAINER.documentation: Persisted form of a model component that contains packages.

packages.name: packages
packages.documentation: Package structure as a hierarchy of packages each potentially containing names of classes in that package in the original model.

cardinality.name: cardinality
cardinality.documentation: Cardinality of this property in its class. Persistent attribute.

type_def.name: type_def
type_def.documentation: Type definition of this property, if not a simple String type reference. Persistent attribute.

type.name: type
type.documentation: If the type is a simple type, then this attribute will hold the type name. If the type is a container or generic, then type_ref will hold the type definition. The resulting type is generated in type_def.

type_ref.name: type_ref
type_ref.documentation: Type definition of this property computed from `_type_` for later use in `_bmm_property_`.

type_ref.name: type_ref
type_ref.documentation: Type definition of this property computed from `_type_` for later use in `_bmm_property_`.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Type definition of this property, if a simple String type reference. Really we should use `_type_def_` to be regular in the schema, but that makes the schema more wordy and less clear. So we use this persisted String value, and compute the `_type_def_` on the fly. Persisted attribute.

type_def.name: type_def
type_def.documentation: Type definition of this property, if not a simple String type reference. Persistent attribute.

P_BMM_ENUMERATION_INTEGER.name: P_BMM_ENUMERATION_INTEGER
P_BMM_ENUMERATION_INTEGER.documentation: Persistent form of an instance of `BMM_ENUMERATION_INTEGER`.

P_BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR.name: P_BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR
P_BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR.documentation: Concrete descendant of `BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR` that provides a way to read an ODIN or other similarly encoded P_BMM schema file.

type_def.name: type_def
type_def.documentation: Type definition of this property, if not a simple String type reference. Persistent attribute.

ASSERTION.name: ASSERTION
ASSERTION.documentation: Meta-type for a first order predicate logic expression with a Boolean result.

tag.name: tag
tag.documentation: Expression tag, used for differentiating multiple assertions.

string_expression.name: string_expression
string_expression.documentation: String form of expression, in case an expression evaluator taking String expressions is used for evaluation.

ASSIGNMENT.name: ASSIGNMENT
ASSIGNMENT.documentation: Meta-type representing the assignment statement, which associates a named variable with an expression, and produces no value.

target.name: target
target.documentation: The target variable on the notional left-hand side of this assignment.

source.name: source
source.documentation: Source right hand side) of the assignment.

EXPR_FUNCTION_CALL.name: EXPR_FUNCTION_CALL
EXPR_FUNCTION_CALL.documentation: Node representing a function call with 0 or more arguments.

arguments.name: arguments
arguments.documentation: Arguments of this function, which can be from 0 to any number. Functions with no arguments are typically used to represent real world varying values like 'current time' and so on.

EXPRESSION.name: EXPRESSION
EXPRESSION.documentation: Abstract parent of all expression meta-types.

type.name: type
type.documentation: The primitive type of this node, which must be determined by redefinitions in concrete classes.

EXPR_LEAF.name: EXPR_LEAF

item.name: item
item.documentation: The reference item from which the value of this node can be computed.

EXPR_LITERAL.name: EXPR_LITERAL
EXPR_LITERAL.documentation: Literal value expression tree leaf item. This can represent a literal value of any primitive type included in the `PRIMITIVE_TYPE` enumeration.

item.name: item
item.documentation: A statically set constant value of a primitive type.

precedence_overridden.name: precedence_overridden
precedence_overridden.documentation: True if the natural precedence of operators is overridden in the expression represented by this node of the expression tree. If True, parentheses should be introduced around the totality of the syntax expression corresponding to this operator node and its operands.

symbol.name: symbol
symbol.documentation: The symbol actually used in the rule, or intended to be used for serialisation. Must be a member of `operator_def.symbols`.

EXPR_VARIABLE_REF.name: EXPR_VARIABLE_REF
EXPR_VARIABLE_REF.documentation: Expression tree leaf item representing a reference to a declared variable.

EXTERNAL_QUERY.name: EXTERNAL_QUERY
EXTERNAL_QUERY.documentation: Definition of a variable whose value is derived from a query run on a data context in the operational environment. Typical uses of this kind of variable are to obtain values like the patient date of birth, sex, weight, and so on. It could also be used to obtain items from a knowledge context, such as a drug database.

context.name: context
context.documentation:
Optional name of context. This allows a basic separation of query types to be done in more sophisticated environments. Possible values might be “patient”, “medications” and so on.
Not yet standardised.

query_id.name: query_id
query_id.documentation:
Identifier of query in the external context, e.g. “date_of_birth”.
Not yet standardised.

query_args.name: query_args
query_args.documentation:
Optional arguments to query.
Not yet standardised.

EXPR_VALUE.name: EXPR_VALUE
EXPR_VALUE.documentation: Any kind of statement element that can be evaluated. The type will either be supplied in descendant types or else will be inferred by an assignment statement linked to a typed variable.

value.name: value
value.documentation: The computed value of this node as a result of the nodes below it, for operator nodes, or else statically set or otherwise derived values.

STATEMENT_SET.name: STATEMENT_SET
STATEMENT_SET.documentation: A container for a specific set of statements intended to be used together.

statement.name: statement
statement.documentation: The member statements of this statement set.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Optional name of this rule set.

execution_result.name: execution_result
execution_result.documentation: Execution result of the whole rule set. Determined by the and-ing of result values of Assertions in the rule set.

STATEMENT.name: STATEMENT
STATEMENT.documentation: Meta-type for the notion of statement, which is a non-value-returning entity.

VARIABLE_DECLARATION.name: VARIABLE_DECLARATION
VARIABLE_DECLARATION.documentation: Meta-type for the declaration of a named variable that can be used in an expression.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Primitive type of the variable, enabling its use to be type-checked in expressions.

EXPR_VALUE_REF.name: EXPR_VALUE_REF
EXPR_VALUE_REF.documentation: Path-based reference to a value in a data structure.

EXPR_FOR_ALL.name: EXPR_FOR_ALL
EXPR_FOR_ALL.documentation: Universal quantification operator, usually known as `for_all`, whose operand is a collection of items referenced by an `EXPR_VALUE_REF`. The `_condition_` attribute represents an assertion that is applied to every member of the collection at runtime to determine the result.

condition.name: condition
condition.documentation: Boolean condition that returns True or False when applied to a member of the operand of a `for_all` operator.

operand.name: operand
operand.documentation: Reference to collection / container object to which the `for_all` operator is to be applied.

OPERATOR_KIND.name: OPERATOR_KIND
item_documentations: Arithemetic exponent (to the power of) operator.

EXPR_TYPE_DEF.name: EXPR_TYPE_DEF
EXPR_TYPE_DEF.documentation: Ancestor class for type definitions known in the openEHR Expression formalism.

type_name.name: type_name
type_name.documentation: Natural language type name of this type as used in abstract rules syntax variable declarations.

type_anchor.name: type_anchor
type_anchor.documentation: Attribute of the openEHR primitive type (or Any) corresponding to this type definition meta-type.

TYPE_DEF_BOOLEAN.name: TYPE_DEF_BOOLEAN
TYPE_DEF_BOOLEAN.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Boolean.

TYPE_DEF_DATE.name: TYPE_DEF_DATE
TYPE_DEF_DATE.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Date.

TYPE_DEF_DATE_TIME.name: TYPE_DEF_DATE_TIME
TYPE_DEF_DATE_TIME.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Date_time.

TYPE_DEF_DURATION.name: TYPE_DEF_DURATION

TYPE_DEF_OBJECT_REF.name: TYPE_DEF_OBJECT_REF
